name: Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina
name_original: Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular desde Constantina
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cazalla De La Sierra are primarily located within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, part of the larger Sierra Morena. The region features rolling hills, evergreen oaks, and a unique dehesa landscape. A significant highlight for touring cyclists is the Vía Verde, a former railway line converted into a cycling path, offering gentle gradients through natural surroundings. The area also includes rivers like the Huéznar and several reservoirs. Best touring cycling routes around Cazalla De La Sierra The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a former railway line, offering a smooth path through the natural landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Isla Margarita Recreation Area – Cerro del Hierro loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a moderate 24.4 miles (39.2 km) path. This route explores diverse terrain, including the Isla Margarita Recreation Area and other natural features. Local touring cyclists also love the Los Castañares Trail – Molino del Corcho Trail loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a 46.1 miles (74.2 km) trail leading through varied woodlands and natural areas, often completed in about 6 hours 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Cazalla De La Sierra is defined by rolling hills, evergreen oaks, and the unique dehesa landscape. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle Vía Verde sections to more challenging routes. The routes in Cazalla De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cazalla De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: Cazalla De La Sierra offers a good selection of touring cycling routes, with over 25 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cazalla De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the peaceful natural surroundings of the Sierra Norte Natural Park, and the unique experience of cycling along the Vía Verde. Many appreciate the blend of rolling hills, evergreen oaks, and the distinctive dehesa landscape.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while touring cycling in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: You can expect a beautiful and varied landscape characterized by rolling hills, ancient evergreen oaks, and lush woods. A significant feature is the 'dehesa,' a unique mixed pasture-woodland landscape. The routes often pass through the Sierra Norte Natural Park, offering stunning scenery, and you'll encounter rivers like the Huéznar and several reservoirs, such as El Pintado.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling options, particularly along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988036636/via-verde-de-la-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-circular-from-constantina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla&lt;/a&gt;. This former railway line offers gentle gradients, making it ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride through olive groves and natural surroundings. While only one route is specifically rated 'easy' in our guide, several 'moderate' routes, like the Vía Verde, are also very accessible.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Cazalla De La Sierra are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988036636/via-verde-de-la-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-circular-from-constantina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 35.8 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Margarita Recreation Area – Cerro del Hierro loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, covering 39.2 km.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes around Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Castañares Trail – Molino del Corcho Trail loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding experience. This route spans 74.2 km with significant elevation changes, taking nearly 7 hours to complete. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31684258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Camino Viejo Bikes Cafeteria loop from Cazalla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 48 km long with over 600 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I see any waterfalls or natural monuments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, particularly near San Nicolás del Puerto. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural monument offering panoramic views. Many routes pass through or near these natural highlights.

Q: Are there any interesting historical sites or cultural attractions to visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Cazalla De La Sierra offers a rich cultural heritage. You can explore the historic Cartuja de Cazalla, a former monastery, or visit the Church of Nuestra Señora de Consolación. The town itself features picturesque whitewashed streets and a charming Plaza Mayor. You might also find historic distilleries like El Clavel and Miura, known for traditional anise and cherry liqueur, providing a glimpse into local traditions.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while cycling in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: The Sierra Norte Natural Park is a haven for wildlife. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, as the area is excellent for bird watching, even offering 'Bird Safari' experiences. The River Huéznar is home to a trout population, and the diverse ecosystems support a range of indigenous species. Keep an eye out for Iberian pigs in the dehesa landscape.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde (Greenway) is particularly suitable for families due to its gentle downhill gradients and smooth path, making it a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. This route allows families to experience the peaceful natural surroundings without encountering challenging terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Spring Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; also offers facilities that can be enjoyed by families.

Q: Where can I find parking for the touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Cazalla De La Sierra start from or pass through towns like Cazalla de la Sierra or Constantina, where you can typically find public parking. For routes like the Vía Verde, specific starting points often have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cazalla De La Sierra?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best times for touring cycling in Cazalla De La Sierra. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, so planning your trip outside of peak summer heat is recommended for the most enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31684258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Camino Viejo Bikes Cafeteria loop from Cazalla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mentions a cafeteria, indicating that such amenities are available. The charming town centers also offer opportunities for breaks and local refreshments.
tips:
The Isla Margarita recreational area is located next to the Hueznar riverbank, accessed via a small wooden bridge. This enclave is of great interest, as in addition to having an exceptional landscape, it is located next to the Vía Verde de la Sierra Morena de Sevilla, one of the most popular and busy trails in the natural park. It is perfect for hiking or cycling. 
The recreational area has a picnic area and a kiosk that offers snacks, drinks and sandwiches (it also has toilets). In addition, there is a hanging forest for the entertainment of the little ones. At the entrance, you will also find an establishment where you can rent bicycles to take the Vía Verde route. 
I hope you have the opportunity to visit it! 🌿🚴‍♂️

An old route conditioned and managed as a greenway by the Junta de Andalucía passes through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, in the Sierra Morena of Seville. The route starts at the Cazalla-Constantina train station and is 19.4 km long. Cultural heritage of the route: old open-cast mine, remains of mining infrastructure, Cerro del Hierro mining town, engineering buildings, administration and civil guard headquarters known locally as "Casas de Ingleses".

It is a beautiful section of the greenway of the Sierra Norte that runs from time to time between trenches.
Sometimes, after heavy rains and until it is cleaned, small landslides with pebbles may appear on the road.

This idyllic town, located in the heart of the Sierra Morena Natural Park, offers the tranquility of the mountains, the pleasure of adventure tourism and the pleasure of its artificial beach. This is a natural pool created by the waters of the Galindón River as it flows through the city.

This old mine, already exploited by the Romans, is a beautiful karstic labyrinth of natural formations and human creations.
You will have to leave your bicycle to visit it, there are a couple of bike racks prepared.
In the town you can regain strength in the wonderful bar before continuing with the route.
